---
title: "Session 6 Reference: System Prompt Components & Constitutional Checklist"
subtitle: "Multi-Agent Orchestration — Generative AI"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: simplex
    css: ../styles.css
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=2cm
    fontsize: 11pt
    toc: false
number-sections: false
---

## System Prompt Components

@mao2025promptstemplatessystematicprompt analysed 2,163 real production prompt templates from company repositories (Uber, Microsoft, LAION-AI and others) and identified seven components with the following frequencies.

| Component | Frequency | What to include |
|---|:---:|---|
| **Directive / Task** | 87% | The core instruction: what the agent should do |
| **Context / Background** | 56% | Information the agent needs to do the task well |
| **Output Format** | 40% | How the response should be structured or formatted |
| **Constraints** | 36% | What the agent must not do, or limits on behaviour |
| **Role / Profile** | 28% | The persona or expertise the agent should adopt |
| **Workflow / Steps** | 28% | Sequential process when task order matters |
| **Examples** | 20% | Demonstrations of the desired output |

Directive, Context, and Output Format appear in the majority of real-world templates. Role appears first in 87% of templates that include it.

---

## Constitutional Checklist

Decide which components are mandatory for your specific agent. Only mandatory components are checked by the judge. Mark each mandatory criterion 1 (satisfied) or 0 (not satisfied).

| Component | Mandatory? (Y/N) | Score (0/1) | Notes |
|---|:---:|:---:|---|
| Directive / Task | | | |
| Context / Background | | | |
| Output Format | | | |
| Constraints | | | |
| Role / Profile | | | |
| Workflow / Steps | | | |
| Examples | | | |
| | **Total** | /  | Approved if all mandatory pass |

---

## Pipeline Structure (today's build)

```
Input (prompt spec dict)
    ↓
[Prompt Advisor Agent]     ← writes system prompt from spec fields
    ↓
[Hard Check]               ← deterministic: length, keyword presence (Python code)
    ↓
┌── Loop (max 3 iterations) ──────────────────────────────┐
│   [Judge Agent]           ← evaluates against constitution│
│       ↓ (if rejected)                                    │
│   [Feedback Agent]        ← proposes specific changes    │
│       ↓                                                  │
│   [Editor Agent]          ← applies changes              │
│       ↓ ──────────────────────────────────────────────→ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ↓ (if approved)
Output: approved system prompt
```

---

## Quick ADK reference

```python
from google.adk.agents import LlmAgent, SequentialAgent, LoopAgent
from google.adk.models.lite_llm import LiteLlm
from google.adk.sessions import InMemorySessionService
from google.adk.runners import Runner
import os

os.environ["OLLAMA_API_BASE"] = "http://localhost:11434"
model = LiteLlm(model="ollama_chat/gemma3:latest")

agent = LlmAgent(name="MyAgent", model=model, instruction="...")
loop  = LoopAgent(name="Loop", sub_agents=[judge, feedback, editor], max_iterations=3)
pipe  = SequentialAgent(name="Pipeline", sub_agents=[advisor, loop])
```

Start the Dev UI: `adk web`

---

## Sources

Mao, Y., He, J., & Chen, C. (2025). From Prompts to Templates: A Systematic Prompt Template Analysis for Real-World LLMApps. arXiv:2504.02052.

Bai, Y. et al. (2022). Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback. arXiv:2212.08073.

Zheng, L. et al. (2023). Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena. NeurIPS 2023. arXiv:2306.05685.
